name: Gene Sedric
description: American jazz clarinetist and tenor saxophonist (1907-1963)
abstract: Eugene Hall Cedric June 17, 1907, St. Louis, Missouri – April 3, 1963, New York City) was an American jazz clarinetist and tenor saxophonist.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: College Jazz Goes to Carnegie Hall
Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Fats had a couple of stalwarts in his band, Gene Sedric, who was the saxophone player... So that kind of – I was thinking about that, back in those days when we were trying to get our little group going, back in St. Louis, back in the ’30s
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Sedric who was with me in Europe... we got a contract to go back to Paris for a tour in Europe... Gene Sedric, Buck Clayton -- all of us got a contract

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer states 'I think Gene Sedric was in that band' and later lists 'Gene' among others in the band. The interviewee (Joe Thomas) does not respond to confirm a personal relationship or membership, merely listening to the interviewer's narrative.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Sedric. I forget who was on drums. Fletcher, he was already in the final stages of his life... Fletcher Henderson came one night and played with a small group... Gene Sedric.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Well, no. You don't get to know them more than you'd see them... everybody's glad to see Sedric and all them.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: he pass out a lead sheet to each one, to me, to Gene Sedric... Sedric, and Al Casey.
